layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN LAYOUT with 2:1 width allocation (left column dominant). The slide uses a clean, professional structure with the title at the top, followed by two side-by-side content containers that fill the remaining vertical space. The left column (Context) receives approximately 65% of the horizontal space to accommodate dense multi-paragraph content, while the right column (Objectives) receives approximately 30% for a compact numbered list. A small gutter (5%) separates the columns. Both columns share identical top and bottom edges, creating strong horizontal alignment. The layout maximizes vertical space utilization with minimal top margin below the title and extends content containers to near the bottom of the slide safe area. No decorative bottom layer is needed as the content fills the slide confidently. Reading flow is natural: title → left column → right column.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.65, 0.3]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT COLUMN — Context): The dominant content container occupying the left two-thirds of the slide body area. This container holds dense multi-paragraph text organized as a structured bullet list with multiple hierarchy levels. The content is presented using native PowerPoint bullets with brand-color bullet markers. The section begins with a bold header 'Context' in brand primary color, followed by a thin solid horizontal line (brand dark color) spanning the full column width as a header underline. Below the header, the content is organized into major bullet points (each representing a time period or theme) with nested sub-bullets (marked with 'o' symbols in the source, rendered as indented brand-color circle bullets). Key phrases within the text are bolded for emphasis (e.g., '2024/2025', 'began the stand-up of our Liquidity Solutions business', '$250-300M of value creation', 'ATLAS'). The text is left-aligned with a small left inset from the container edge. Vertical spacing between major bullet sections is minimal due to content density — thin dashed grey separator lines are placed between major bullet groups to create visual rhythm without consuming vertical space. The container background is white with no border or fill. Font size is set to body font size (medium) to ensure all content fits comfortably within the allocated space without overflow. The container stretches vertically to fill all available space between the title area and the slide bottom margin, with content distributed evenly using flexGrow on major bullet sections.
  C1 (RIGHT COLUMN — Objectives): A compact secondary container occupying the right third of the slide body area, aligned as an equal-peer with the left column (identical top and bottom edges). This container holds a numbered list of three objectives. The section begins with a bold header 'Objectives' in brand primary color, followed by a thin solid horizontal line (brand dark color) spanning the full column width as a header underline. Below the header, the three objectives are presented as an enumeration list with numbered badges. Each objective row contains a small filled square badge (brand primary color, small corner radius, white bold number centered: '1.', '2.', '3.') positioned at the left edge of the row, vertically centered. The objective text sits to the right of each badge, left-aligned, using body font in brand text color. Key phrases within each objective are bolded (e.g., 'Review outcomes from ATLAS Phase 1', 'Demo ATLAS Phase 1 solution', 'discuss funding requirements for ATLAS Phase 2'). Time allocations '[10 mins]' appear at the end of each objective in regular weight. Thin dashed grey horizontal separator lines are placed between the three objective rows (not after the last one) to create visual rhythm and align with the separator pattern in the left column. The container background is white with no border or fill. Vertical space is distributed evenly across the three objective rows using equal flexGrow values, ensuring the rows align proportionally with the major sections in the left column where possible. The container stretches vertically to match the full height of the left column, creating strong horizontal alignment across the slide.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BOTTOM LAYER — Inactive): Not active. The slide content (two columns with dense text) fills the available space confidently. No visual grouping or background fill is needed. The white slide background provides sufficient contrast and the content containers define their own boundaries through alignment and spacing.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER — Inactive): Not active. No cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements are needed for this slide. The two columns are independent content sections with no visual links between them.

_reference_violations.description: C1 (Objectives): text block occupies approximately 35-40% of container inner height — severely under-filled. The three numbered objective rows with their badges and text consume less than half the vertical space of the container, leaving a large empty white band below objective #3 that extends to the container bottom edge.

C0 (Context) and C1 (Objectives) do not share identical bottom edges. The Context container extends significantly lower on the slide than the Objectives container, violating the layout description requirement that 'both columns share identical top and bottom edges, creating strong horizontal alignment'.

_reference_critique: C1 (Objectives): text block occupies approximately 35-40% of container height — severely under-filled. More than half the container is empty white space below the third objective. SVGSketcher must increase the body font size for the objective text from the current size toward 18pt (or the maximum allowed body font ceiling) until the three objective rows collectively fill at least 65% of the container inner height. Additionally, increase the vertical spacing (margin/padding) between the numbered badge rows to distribute content more evenly across the available vertical space. The numbered badges should also scale proportionally with the increased font size to maintain visual balance. Apply consistent font sizing to C0 body text to preserve within-slide uniformity.
